sources.name: Strunk & White, Elements of Style — Rule 13: Omit Needless Words
sources.note: Public domain. Verb-to-noun constructions inflate sentence length and distance the actor from the action. Every word must tell.

sources.name: Strunk & White, Elements of Style — Rule 10: Use the Active Voice
sources.note: The verb carries the action. When action is buried in a noun, the verb becomes a placeholder.

sources.name: Purdue OWL — Active Verb Tenses
sources.note: Active constructions keep actors and actions visible.

sources.name: Williams, J. — Style: Lessons in Clarity and Grace
sources.note: Nominalization chapter — verb-to-noun constructions as primary source of inflated, unclear prose.

_description: Verb-to-noun constructions (nominalizations) that inflate sentence length and hide the actor. Each entry gives the nominalized form, its direct verb replacement, and the rule ID. Replace without exception unless domain_exceptions.json permits the term.
_rule: When a nominalized form is found in output, replace it with the direct verb form. No exceptions unless domain tag is active and domain_exceptions.json explicitly permits the term.

blocklist.example_before: The committee will make a decision by Friday.
blocklist.example_after: The committee will decide by Friday.

blocklist.example_before: The agency will provide assistance to affected residents.
blocklist.example_after: The agency will help affected residents.

blocklist.example_before: The team will conduct an analysis of the data.
blocklist.example_after: The team will analyze the data.

blocklist.example_before: Investigators reached the conclusion that the cause was structural.
blocklist.example_after: Investigators concluded the cause was structural.

blocklist.example_before: The decision will have an impact on all residents.
blocklist.example_after: The decision will affect all residents.

blocklist.example_before: The board should give consideration to the proposal.
blocklist.example_after: The board should consider the proposal.

blocklist.example_before: Staff will make an assessment of the damage.
blocklist.example_after: Staff will assess the damage.

blocklist.example_before: Guidance with respect to safety protocols has changed.
blocklist.example_after: Guidance on safety protocols has changed.

blocklist.example_before: The panel will provide a recommendation next week.
blocklist.example_after: The panel will recommend next week.

blocklist.example_before: Police will carry out an investigation into the incident.
blocklist.example_after: Police will investigate the incident.

blocklist.example_before: The team made an attempt to reach the survivors.
blocklist.example_after: The team tried to reach the survivors.

blocklist.example_before: Leadership must take action immediately.
blocklist.example_after: Leadership must act immediately.

blocklist.example_before: The agency will provide support to the affected population.
blocklist.example_after: The agency will support the affected population.

blocklist.example_before: The minister gave approval for the operation.
blocklist.example_after: The minister approved the operation.

blocklist.example_before: Contractors must make a payment by the deadline.
blocklist.example_after: Contractors must pay by the deadline.

blocklist.example_before: The auditor will perform a review of the accounts.
blocklist.example_after: The auditor will review the accounts.

blocklist.example_before: This change will have an effect on all contracts.
blocklist.example_after: This change will affect all contracts.

blocklist.example_before: Both parties reached an agreement on the terms.
blocklist.example_after: Both parties agreed on the terms.

blocklist.example_before: The delegate made a request for more time.
blocklist.example_after: The delegate asked for more time.

blocklist.example_before: The department will provide notification to all staff.
blocklist.example_after: The department will notify all staff.

blocklist.example_before: Each team member made a contribution to the report.
blocklist.example_after: Each team member contributed to the report.

blocklist.example_before: The institute will undertake a study of the region.
blocklist.example_after: The institute will study the region.

blocklist.example_before: The spokesperson gave an explanation of the delay.
blocklist.example_after: The spokesperson explained the delay.

blocklist.example_before: Teams should make use of available resources.
blocklist.example_after: Teams should use available resources.

blocklist.example_before: The office is in receipt of the documents.
blocklist.example_after: The office received the documents.

blocklist.nominalization: it is important to note that
blocklist.example_before: It is important to note that the deadline has passed.
blocklist.example_after: The deadline has passed.
blocklist.note: This phrase delays the point without adding emphasis. Delete it and state the point.

blocklist.example_before: Staff must sign in order to access the system.
blocklist.example_after: Staff must sign to access the system.

blocklist.example_before: The flight was cancelled due to the fact that weather conditions were poor.
blocklist.example_after: The flight was cancelled because weather conditions were poor.
blocklist.source: Strunk & White Rule 13 — owing to the fact that → since / because

blocklist.nominalization: in spite of the fact that
blocklist.example_before: In spite of the fact that the report was late, work continued.
blocklist.example_after: Although the report was late, work continued.

blocklist.example_before: The utilization of available tools is required.
blocklist.example_after: Using available tools is required.
